title: sida azorica
intro: Sida azorica, also known as the Azores mallow, is a plant species that belongs to the mallow family, characterized by its pink to purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, Sida azorica has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties, such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects. In herbal preparations, Sida azorica is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to promote overall health and well-being, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
